💥💥💥** How Zionists tried courting the Third Reich to 'liberate' Palestine**

Another dark page of the history of Zionism has recently emerged — the attempts of the Zionist Lehi movement to work with the German Third Reich to "liberate" Palestine, Haazerz newspaper writes, citing declassified documents:

🔴 The Zionist Lehi movement, also know as the Stern Gang, was created after splitting from another Zionist organization, Irgun, led by Abraham Stern.

🔴 Stern believed the Second World War was no obstacle to fighting against the British in Palestine, and used terrorism to advance his goals

🔴 Having gathered a few hundred men, he then tried reaching out to Adolf Hitler, thinking the Nazis would allow him to recruit to the Lehi organization and create an Israeli state in Palestine

🔴 To put that into practice, the Stern Gang sent several envoys to the Germans, though the contacts didn’t succeed

🔴 "Stern argued that there is no better time for a war of independence than during wartime. The question of orientation seemed simple to him," one of the declassified files writes.

🔴 Stern himself wasn’t the only Zionist leader seeking an alliance with the Third Reich. "Germany has not yet been defeated and may still become our ally," future member of the Knesset Natan Friedman wrote in 1934

full_text:
😰🇺🇸🪖 **Silicon Valley leased infrastructure to IDF to help Netanyahu bomb Palestinians**

In 2021, Google's lawyers warned in an internal report that its cloud services could be used for human rights violations in Palestinian territories. The exact phrasing: "Google Cloud services could be used for, or linked to, the facilitation of human rights violations, including Israeli activity in the West Bank."  

👉 Top management noted it and signed the [Project Nimbus](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/68045) contract anyway — a 7 year deal with an option to extend. The $1.2 billion agreement covers every branch of Israel's government. The Ministry of Defense is the largest single customer, accounting for roughly $525 million. 

The contract mandates data storage on local servers under Israeli jurisdiction, though it preserves Google's right to suspend service for terms-of-service violations. 

🔊 Publicly, Google denied any military dimension to Nimbus, insisting the project was "not directed at highly sensitive, classified, or military workloads relevant to weapons or intelligence services" — all while servicing Israel's Ministry of Defense.  

The deal legally obligates the tech giants to serve private weapons manufacturers, including [Rafael](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/66523?single) and Israel Aerospace Industries. 

🚀 Rafael produces Spike missiles and SPICE guidance kits — the technology that turns unguided bombs into precision munitions. SPICE components have been recovered from the rubble of mass casualty strikes in Gaza, including a bombing of a refugee camp that the UN condemned as "unconscionable." 

The infrastructure directly powers [Israel's AI targeting systems,](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/66054) "Lavender" and "The Gospel," both developed by the military's elite intelligence Unit 8200.  Lavender has flagged over 37,000 Palestinians as potential targets. Human operators then spent as little as 20 seconds confirming each strike. 

🛩 In March 2026, Iranian Shahed drones struck [Amazon data centers](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/68022) in the UAE and Bahrain — the first direct hits of their kind, disrupting cloud services across the region.  

US corporate infrastructure is now a legitimate military target. Private server farms have become indistinguishable from weapons factories.

👍 **US-Israel-Iran war | ****@geopolitics_prime**
headline: 😰🇺🇸🪖 **Silicon Valley leased infrastructure to IDF to help Netanyahu bomb Palestinians**

full_text:
**🚨****🇵🇰 Why Pakistan is vested in stopping Iran war **

Behind Pakistan’s flurry of mediatory efforts in the US war on Iran sits a secret defense commitment to Saudi Arabia that could drag it into the conflict, reveals Drop Site News.

👉 Pakistan’s security relationship with Saudi Arabia has quietly evolved from training exchanges into a long-classified mutual defense framework, repeatedly revised since the 1980s, leaked documents show.

Most recently it was upgraded into a Strategic Mutual Defense Agreement signed in September 2025, to commit Pakistan "to support the armed forces of [Saudi Arabia] in dealing with any threat that affects its security, safety, sovereignty, territorial integrity and interests."   

‼️ Inside Pakistan’s political system, the deal largely bypassed democratic scrutiny, and the final iterations were purportedly signed under caretaker administrations. 

Internal assessments ostensibly flagged unresolved issues such as whether nuclear assets were implicitly included (they were not intended to be) and whether Pakistan could be forced into conflicts beyond Saudi borders.

🤝 The secret agreement loads Pakistan with broad, open-ended security obligations — but Saudi Arabia is not bound to mirror those commitments with comparable military guarantees, underscores the outlet. 

Instead, the relationship tilts into financial stabilization. Over the years, Saudi Arabia has repeatedly stepped in to support Pakistan’s fragile economy, including maintaining more than $5 billion in deposits at the State Bank of Pakistan — funds that are routinely rolled over.

🗣 After it was signed, the expanded wording that deepened Pakistan’s commitments to Saudi Arabia triggered intense debate within the military establishment, where officials reportedly argued over just how far those obligations should stretch and what risks they actually locked Pakistan into.

Any direct confrontation with Iran would risk internal blowback, regional encirclement, and the collapse of Pakistan’s balancing act between Gulf allies and Iran.
Back in 2015, Saudi Arabia asked Pakistan to join its war in Yemen, yet Pakistan’s parliament refused.

📑 Today, no such clear parliamentary firewall exists for the newer defense framework, notes the publication.
Amid the Iran escalation, Pakistani forces and aircraft have been deployed to Saudi territory under the pact, officially framed as “operational readiness.” 

The pact remains defensive on paper, and no authorization exists for offensive action from Pakistani soil – for now.

👍 **US-Israel-Iran war | ****@geopolitics_prime**
headline: **🚨****🇵🇰 Why Pakistan is vested in stopping Iran war **

full_text:
🇺🇸🇮🇷 **Iran flashpoint: US attempts to target BRICS & multipolarity
**
The US war with Iran carries different goals that even President Donald Trump cannot fully frame in one way.

🤔 However, beyond the political theatre, the bigger picture may be a strategic effort to disrupt the foundations of an emerging multipolar order — and reshape the global balance of power in America’s favour, analysts say.

Unable to compete directly with emerging global powers — 🇨🇳China and 🇷🇺Russia — the US instead seeks to slow their ascent, dragging down rivals to preserve its dominance, including by trying to weaken Iran.

🛢 Striking Iran also threatens vital energy flows from the Middle East, destabilising supply chains that much of the developing and Eurasian world depends on.

The knock-on effects:** **Rising energy costs and potential economic slowdown across large regions — reducing demand for Chinese exports while simultaneously constraining China’s access to energy.

But China and Russia are not easy targets.

💪 **BRICs unbreakable **

China already developed workarounds to maintain oil flows — diversifying imports via suppliers such as Brazil and Russia, while reducing reliance on the Middle East, whose share of its oil supply has declined over time.

It also continued to import discounted Iranian crude, with payments often routed through yuan-based mechanisms and shadow shipping networks designed to bypass sanctions.

At the same time, Beijing built up substantial strategic reserves and relied on smaller “teapot” refineries to keep sanctioned crude moving through alternative channels.

Russia, meanwhile, increased exports to China and benefited from rerouted global energy flows, as sanctioned crude finds alternative buyers.

Despite disruption, China’s imports have not collapsed — supported by stockpiles, diversified supply routes, and flexible logistics that absorb external shocks.

🤔 **The real ‘victim’**

In the US attempt to undermine BRICS, ironically, it is neither Russia nor China that suffer most — instead, the burden is carried by US allies in Europe.

“The cheapest energy is the energy you don’t use,” says Ursula von der Leyen, urging Europeans to stay at home, avoid driving, and reduce electricity consumption.

__While the war creates volatility, it does not decisively halt the rise of BRICS or multipolarity.

full_text:
⚠️🇮🇷 **How Iran’s Qeshm & Abu Musa lock down Hormuz
**
The rugged terrain of Iran’s Qeshm Island — the largest island in the Persian Gulf and a core IRGC stronghold – has turned it into a natural bunker system.

🔴 Its mountain ranges double as hardened shields for underground missile facilities designed to survive and relocate firepower.

🔴 A dispersed network of mobile anti-ship launchers offers coastal coverage aimed at controlling movement through the Strait of Hormuz.

🔴 Packed with drone launch facilities and radar arrays, it serves as the strategic backbone of Iran’s coastal defense network.

Abu Musa Island sits closer to the Gulf’s southern approaches, functioning more like a heavily fortified tripwire, complete with:

🔴 early-warning radar coverage scanning deep into Gulf traffic lanes

🔴 forward-positioned surveillance over military and commercial vessels

The result is a full-spectrum anti-access/area-denial zone extending deep into the Gulf.

👍 **US-Israel-Iran war | ****@geopolitics_prime**
headline: ⚠️🇮🇷 **How Iran’s Qeshm & Abu Musa lock down Hormuz

full_text:
**🚨**** Trump's Iran blockade could backfire — Saudis warn as Houthis eye Red Sea shipping**

Saudi officials are warning that a blockade on Iranian ports could backfire, triggering wider global energy disruption, according to media reports.

👉 Trump aims to pressure Iran over the Strait of Hormuz. But Saudi Arabia fears the Bab al-Mandeb — handling about 10% of global crude and LNG — may also be threatened, and is urging the US to return to negotiations.

The kingdom has kept near pre-conflict exports by rerouting crude to Red Sea ports, bypassing Hormuz. That alternative route would become vulnerable if Iran retaliates by disrupting other shipping lanes. Its Houthi allies in Yemen control territory near the Bab al-Mandeb and have struck maritime traffic before.

📈 The result: higher insurance costs, ship rerouting, supply delays — and higher energy prices.

👍 **US-Israel-Iran war | ****@geopolitics_prime**
headline: **🚨**** Trump's Iran blockade could backfire — Saudis warn as Houthis eye Red Sea shipping**

**🚨****🇮🇷**** Iranian delegation felt threatened after abrupt end to US talks - Prof. Marandi**

🔊 When JD Vance pulled the plug on talks with Iran in Pakistan, this coincided with an opinion piece in the CIA-affiliated Washington Post that called for "a final barrage of leadership strikes," says Tehran-based analyst Professor Mohammad Marandi. 

In his opinion, this “sent a message” to the Iranian delegation, as security concerns escalated. 

💬 “They secretly got into a different plane, and that plane on the way to Tehran suddenly diverted and landed very swiftly in Mashhad. And then we all came to Tehran by train, by car, and by bus.”

He adds that no one wanted to stay in Pakistan, and everyone was willing to take the risk, “just like the Iranians on the streets who, during the missile attacks, remained steadfast on the streets.”

🚨🇮🇱** IDF fired on Gaza aid seekers for no reason – GHF whistleblower**

Israeli soldiers often shot at Gaza Humanitarian Foundation contractors and "used stray dogs for target practice," says David McIntosh, a former contractor with Safe Reach Solutions, which ran logistics for the GHF.

👉 The IDF used deadly force against aid seekers who posed no threat, the whistleblower reveals, and describes an incident when an Israeli sniper shot a 12-year-old boy, whom he believes was fatally wounded.

McIntosh says he reported what he'd witnessed at the aid distribution centers to his higher-ups, but they never even followed up.

👍 **US-Israel-Iran war | ****@geopolitics_prime**
headline: 🚨🇮🇱** IDF fired on Gaza aid seekers for no reason – GHF whistleblower**

full_text:
🚨🔥 **China's Belt and Road shatters US blockade of Iran — analyst**

National security analyst Brandon Weichert says Washington's naval blockade will fail. Iran can simply ship oil overland to China — its main customer — via rebuilt railways. Israel bombed one key line, but [it's already restored.](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/68066)

"It is not inconceivable that if we did make this blockade serious, they'd transport energy overland into Central Asia, into China, and the Chinese would keep flowing in weapons to help Iran resist us."

The US empire is outmaneuvered.

👍 **US-Israel-Iran war | ****@geopolitics_prime**
headline: 🚨🔥 **China's Belt and Road shatters US blockade of Iran — analyst**

🔥🤡 **Detour of SHAME — US carrier group sails around Africa to avoid Houthi hypersonic missiles**

Instead of transiting the Red Sea and Bab el-Mandeb, the USS George H.W. Bush and its escorts — including the USS Donald Cook, USS Mason, and USS Ross — are skirting the entire continent, USNI News reported.

The world's "most powerful navy" has been rerouted by Yemen.

headline: 🔥🤡 **Detour of SHAME — US carrier group sails around Africa to avoid Houthi hypersonic missiles**

full_text:
🗣 **Allies fear Trump more than adversaries — and that's killing US power projection**

Prof. John Mearsheimer puts it bluntly: US allies fear Trump more than the nations they want to coerce.

"Leaders go to great lengths not to provoke him. But in private, they think he is positively dangerous," he said.

When allies start hiding their true thoughts, the superpower sails half-blind.

"This is a disastrous situation for our ability to project power all over the world."

👍 **US-Israel-Iran war | ****@geopolitics_prime**
headline: 🗣 **Allies fear Trump more than adversaries — and that's killing US power projection**
